target.title: fix(cli): bridge inter-tool commentary events to channel progress
target.body:
## Summary

This is the **general CLI-dispatch commentary _delivery_ layer** — the missing subscriber in the shared CLI-runtime agent dispatch (`runCliAgentWithLifecycle`). It is **not claude-specific**.

Inter-tool **commentary** (the assistant text a model emits *before* a tool call — "Let me check X…", "Found it, now reading Y…") surfaces on the agent-event bus as a `stream:"item", kind:"preamble"` event. The CLI dispatch already bridged the **assistant**, **reasoning**, and **tool** streams to the channel, but the **item/preamble** stream had **no subscriber** — so commentary landed on the bus and was never delivered to the channel's commentary render hook.

This adds the missing **`createCommentaryEventBridge`** (mirroring `createToolEventBridge`): it subscribes to the `item`/`preamble` stream and forwards it to `onItemEvent`, so commentary reaches the channel render hook (`onItemEvent` → `pushCommentaryProgress`, already on `main`).

### Scope: this is the delivery half, not a per-backend producer

This PR makes the CLI-runtime dispatch *deliver* commentary; it does **not** by itself make any backend *emit* it. Producers are per-backend:

- It applies to **every CLI-runtime backend on this dispatch** — `claude-cli`, `google-gemini-cli`, and any future CLI backend — i.e. it's backend-agnostic infrastructure.
- It brings the CLI-runtime dispatch to **parity with codex**, whose ACP parent stream (`acp-spawn-parent-stream`) already relays `phase:"commentary"` text. Codex uses that separate path and is unaffected by this change.
- The first **producer** on this dispatch is **claude-cli**, added by **#89834**. With #89834 present, claude-cli commentary flows producer → this bridge → render.

So by itself this branch is the delivery infrastructure; paired with #89834 it produces the user-visible fix for #87326.

### Wired into both CLI dispatch call sites

- **Primary run** (`agent-runner-execution.ts`) — `onCommentaryText` → `onItemEvent({ kind: "preamble", … })`
- **Queued follow-up run** (`followup-runner.ts`) — `onCommentaryText` → `forwardFollowupProgressEvent` as an `item`/`preamble` event, using the same `emitChannelProgress`/`detailMode` gates as the existing `onToolEvent` so silent/verbose behavior is preserved

(Wiring only the primary path would leave commentary dropped for queued follow-up turns — both `runCliAgentWithLifecycle` call sites need it.)

## Tests

This is delivery-layer plumbing, so the unit tests assert the bridge forwards `item`/`preamble` events to `onItemEvent` (both call sites). End-to-end behavior — a real parser producing commentary that this bridge delivers and the channel renders — is exercised by the **combined stack** (this + #89834) and shown in the proof below.

- `tsgo` clean
- `bridges CLI commentary agent events into onItemEvent for live preview` (primary path)
- `bridges queued CLI inter-tool commentary into onItemEvent for live preview` (follow-up path)

## Proof (combined stack: this delivery bridge + #89834 producer)

Captured via a **local recreation of the Mantis (telegram-desktop-proof) flow on crabbox** — same capture mechanism Mantis uses (Telegram Desktop + user-driver + screen capture), run against `this + #89834` on `upstream/main`. Commentary is transient (no persist), so the proof is the during-turn recording, not the after-turn still.

The live progress draft renders the inter-tool commentary (claude-cli's "I'll read each file and summarize it." appearing as live progress before the tool calls):

![claude-cli inter-tool commentary in the live Telegram progress draft](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/cli-commentary-87326/cli-commentary-proof.png)

Animated (progress draft building → final answer): [motion capture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/cli-commentary-87326/cli-commentary-motion.gif)

The observed draft text:

> **Tidepooling**
> _I'll read each file and summarize it._

i.e. claude-cli commentary now appears in the channel progress draft (producer #89834 → this bridge → render), instead of being dropped on the bus.

## Issues

Part of the fix for #87326 (delivery half; **depends on #89834** for the claude-cli producer).
Related: #84486 (Feishu — same CLI-dispatch delivery gap; its render path is separate).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
